description: Detect unusual spikes, drops, or outliers in learning and compliance metrics over time. Traces upstream through causal graph to determine whether anomalies originate locally or propagate from upstream drivers.

transformations: compute rolling baseline (mean, stddev) over configurable window
compute z-score deviation from baseline per time period
group anomalies by focus_area to identify systemic vs isolated issues
trace upstream through causal graph to identify propagation source

cce_config.uses: upstream traversal from anomalous metric to identify whether the anomaly originates locally or propagates from an upstream driver

recommender_instructions.metric_selection_bias: ONLY select trend metrics with time-series data; reject current_state-only metrics; prefer metrics with sufficient history for baseline computation (>= 4 periods)
recommender_instructions.output_guidance: each recommended metric MUST include: metric_type='trend', time_window for baseline computation, deviation_threshold (sigma), and a natural_language_question that queries the metric's time-series. Include upstream_drivers (from causal graph) for each metric.
recommender_instructions.causal_usage: for each anomalous metric, walk upstream through causal edges to find the originating driver; distinguish 'local anomaly' (no upstream anomaly) from 'propagated anomaly' (upstream driver also anomalous)

penalty_rules: penalize current_state-only metrics with no trend capability (-0.25)
penalize metrics with insufficient time-series history (< 4 periods) (-0.10)
penalize metrics with no causal graph connections for upstream tracing (-0.05)
boost_rules: boost metrics with rich time-series data (>= 12 periods) (+0.10)
boost metrics connected to multiple downstream metrics in causal graph (+0.08)
boost metrics aligned with known volatility areas (+0.05)
